summary: Close the C5 compatibility and serialization gap that rejected structurally valid Customizer vectors longer than four components.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.topic: Customizer vectors longer than four components
decisions.decision: Treat every nonempty finite numeric vector as serializable and require compatibility against the source default by exact component count, without an application-owned maximum component count. Give panel inputs beyond the conventional x/y/z/w axes stable index-qualified keys.
decisions.rationale: The structural parser and Customizer contract already accept vectors longer than four values. The obsolete schema ceiling made valid parsed controls fail during edits, exact source rewriting, and named-set export; removing only that ceiling aligns every C5 path while preserving the existing nonempty, exact-length, finite-number, file-size, and hostile-input safeguards.

testEvidence.criterion: Six-component vectors traverse schema, live state, source rewriting, stock set interchange, and panel input without weakening safety checks
testEvidence.test: Assert acceptance and serialization of six finite values; edit/save/reset/reapply through parameter state; preserve unchanged source component tokens while rewriting tail values; export/import the exact stock JSON string; interact with the sixth panel input; and retain rejection of empty, wrong-length, nonfinite, hostile, and malformed values.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 4 failed | 1 passed (5); Tests 5 failed | 40 passed (45). Schema acceptance returned false, state editing and exact rewrite rejected the value as incompatible, stock-set encoding raised ParameterSetFormatError, and the safety-message assertion exposed the obsolete one-to-four wording. The six-input panel interaction passed independently.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 45 passed (45); Duration 1.53s.

questions: The shared parser and rendered controls already accepted vectors longer than four, so parser-only or render-only tests could not detect the downstream schema ceiling.
Inputs after the conventional x/y/z/w axes shared one React key; index-qualified fallback keys now preserve stable reconciliation for vectors with six or more components.
